raw_input:
[4.0/5] My skin feels good This Shiny Leaf combo of skin care helps my skin feel softer and looking fresh. I have very sensitive skin and have not experienced any irritation or breakouts. The consistency is thicker, kind of gel or creamy depending on the item. This helps transfer to your skin without it slipping through your fingers. I use 1-2 pumps for my face and neck, moving down to my chest if any is left. I think they add good ingredients to my skin. I follow up with a moisturizing night cream. I am 65 and think these serums help. [5.0/5] Great My skin feels moisturized, tight, and soft after using these. I love the pumps. These kind of products usually have droppers and these work so much better. I think the price for these is really great also. I like how easy the instructions are. [5.0/5] Great serum set with easy application I like this set of facial serums. The packaging of the product makes all the difference. These are glass bottles but instead of the annoying dropper cap, these serums are a gel-like consistency that is pumped out instead. No dripping serums wasted. It does have a bland scent which I'm not too crazy about but that's just from smelling it from the bottle. I can't smell it when it's on my face which is good. The serums all absorb quickly into my skin and they don't feel greasy. I haven't experienced any adverse skin reaction. After application I can see my skin glowing, when I first used it my skin became a little pink but it went away quickly. It was probably just my skin adjusting to the retinol and it doesn't happen anymore since I've been using it for a few nights now. I'm pretty happy with this set and it's reasonably priced if you're just starting to venture into the anti-aging skin care regimen. [5.0/5] A Triple Threat I've used these 3 serums in combination for a few years now. They are great for helping repair skin damaged from the sun or other elements. This combination is good for oily or dry skin, and when used with a good toner and moisturizer, can be a great daily skin regiment.

I use the vitamin c immediately after washing and toning my face. I rub it all over my face, chin and neck. I usually wait a few minutes before using the retinol serum followed by the hyaluronic acid. After I'm done, I finish with a good moisturizer to mitigate any dryness that may occur. My skin is usually oily, so I use a moisturizer with a good sun screen and oil controller.

The results are great, and once you start noticing the improvements you won't stray from the process. It's awesome to find great products at a good price. The Shiny Leaf brand delivers. [5.0/5] Great value I was mainly interested in using this because it was such a great value and seemed interesting and for the price it's definitely worth trying. You get three products with this set three full sized bottles of retinol, hyaluronic acid, and vitamin c each of these are essential for your skin and are a great addition to any skin acne routine so you can use everything included. The serums themselves are of a decent quality and seem comparable to other serums but at times they do feel somewhat cheaper this is especially apparent when you feel the plastic bottles and in my case when I need to apply more product than I used to in order to reach all the areas of my face I like to cover but even with these issues it's still worth it you can spend the entire cost of this set on just one of these serums if you go with certain brands so there's definitely a value here and if you're ok getting a cheaper feeling bottle and having to use a little more product this is a great way to save money in all honesty I've tried lots of serums and they all seem to be almost identical in terms of performance so as long as you don't get a adverse reaction from a specific brand you can use whichever brand you like. [5.0/5] Excellent product / 10/10 Absolutely amazing tonight was my first time trying them and they’re great
The after feel of your face is amazing very smooth and moisturizing I will continue using these products and I will be purchasing again when I’m out [1.0/5] It irritated my skin Updated review: I wanted to come back and update this after more use. One of these or all three of them have really irritated my skin for some reason. I don't know why, but maybe I'm allergic to something in them? It has really dried out my face to a painful extent, and my skin is red and flaky. I don't even know how to fix my skin from this because it hurts to put any products on it. I wish I knew more about skincare for this exact reason, but hopefully my skin recovers after a few days of not using this product.

Original review: I am definitely a beginner when it comes to skincare, so I like this pack because it tells you exactly how to use the products (bottle 1 goes on in the morning, and you use bottles 2 and 3 at night). That makes it a really easy way for me to start getting acquainted with skincare. All three bottles have a pump top, and the texture of the liquid is kind of thicker than I thought it would be, like aloe or shampoo instead of oil or water consistency. That makes it easier to get it from the bottle to my face, at least for me. Now, the smells of these products are very strange/slightly on the unpleasant side. Like I would be a lot more happy putting a better-smelling or scent-free product on my face than these. I can't exactly describe what the smell is, but it's just something I don't like. Oh well. It takes about four pumps of each one to cover my face entirely, and it does leave my skin feeling refreshed and smooth. They all dry without any greasy or sticky feeling, which is a huge plus. I can't exactly be sure if they're working, since I'm not a skincare expert, but they definitely feel hydrating at the very least. [5.0/5] A great find! Love it. This is my new regime. Scent free, yet moisturizing. [4.0/5] Generous size, not sure about concentration These serums feel good on my skin and I have also been mixing them with my moisturizer. I’m not sure about the concentration of their active ingredients, and it does not say anything specific on the box or the bottle. I know that I have a different vitamin C serum that consistently creates a warming sensation on my skin, but this serum does not do that so it must be below 12%. [5.0/5] I can already see a difference in my skin! This is my first adventure with these kind of skincare products. I had heard of retinol and hyaluronic acid and always thought they would be extremely expensive. Theses products are at a great price. I like that they are made in the USA, are paraben, sulfate, and chemical free - and are never tested on animals.

Shiny Leaf Skin Pro Series:
1. Vitamin C Serum
2. Retinol Serum
3. Hyaluronic Acid Serum

I have very sensitive skin and these serums do not bother my skin at all. I really like the way they go on my skin and how they leave my skin looking. The scent is very mild and pleasant. My daughter told me she can already see the difference in my skin!

I use them the way my daughter told me to use them.

First I wash my face with a mild skin cleanser, then I apply the Retinol Serum first, then the Hyaluronic Acid Serum second, then the vitamin C Serum third. Layering them on top of each other. In the morning I wash them off and then apply the Hyaluronic Acid Serum first, and then the Vitamin C Serum second - I do not use the Retinol Serum in the morning. I only use a small amount of each and rub it into my face, neck, and upper chest. Then continue to do my morning routine of my moisturizer that has sunscreen in it, and my foundation makeup that has sun screen in it, and the rest of my make up. My daughter told me to be sure to wear sunscreen when I go outside because the retinol can make my skin sensitive to the sun. I did test the products on a small area of my skin one night and one morning to make sure I did not have any allergies to the serums.

When I first started using the serums it took me a few pumps to get the serum out of the bottle which is typical of a never used product. I suggest when you push the pump each time to hold it at an angle toward your finger tip so the product does not over squirt and get wasted.

I plan to continue using these serums and will purchase more.

I will update if there are any changes. [5.0/5] Quite happy with the whole set I’m happy with this Shiny Leaf set of skin care serums. I’m familiar with hyaluronic acid as I use it regularly. This serum is a bit thicker than the one I used previously. I can’t say that I prefer either. It’s just different. I always apply hyaluronic acid when my face is a bit wet. Otherwise it can be drying. It does make sense to me that the directions indicate that the retinol should be used at the same time as the hyaluronic acid. Retinol can be drying on its own. Especially if you’re not used to it. I do not find this one to be drying for the most part. I am applying a moisturizer on top of both serums before bed and a moisturizer with spf on top of the vitamin c serum in the morning. Back to the retinol… I did try it on my eyelids once (being cautious not to get it in my eyes). I did get quite a dry patch on one eyelid. I do occasionally get eczema on that eyelid specifically, so there’s some sort of issue there anyway. I can’t blame the serum, but I would suggest being cautious and starting slowly if you have any skin sensitivities. The vitamin C serum goes on nicely, but this one irritates my skin for a bit after application. I get very small bumps on my face similar to what I get on my arms when I do yard work. It will itch and feel warm for a bit as well. Since it cleared up without issue the first time I tried it, I’ve continued to use it. This is my first experience with vitamin C on my skin, so I cannot speak to whether or not I have this reaction with all such serums. I suspect that I would, though. Compared to other serums I’ve purchased this set seems to be a good value for the money. I like the pump and the amount dispensed with each pump is great. The bottles are glass rather than plastic. I appreciate that as well. [5.0/5] Fabulous I’ve used several brands of these, all of which were fine and do the job, making my face smoother, more youthful, more even skin tones. This particular brand is the best I’ve used. The reason is the viscosity. All the other brands I’ve tried were watery and dripped down my face when I apply. This brand is thicker, kind of an aloe Vera gel consistency which I really like. Not only that but for the same price, I get twice the product. I will def buy this brand again. [5.0/5] Pleased! My face feels smooth and hydrated. Dryness is gone! [5.0/5] I recommend this as an affordable option. All the serums are thicker, almost gel like, compared to what I'm used to. They all absorb quickly and smoothly and should work well under makeup.

The vitamin C is clear and has almost no scent except for maybe the faintest hint of sweet almond. I wouldn't call it "ultra-brightening", but it produces an average glow.

The hyaluronic is cloudy, has a slight "not fun" smell, and didn't feel like it moisturized.

The retinol is beige and has a slight alcohol smell in the bottle but isn't noticeable when applying. It didn't feel like it moisturized either. It made me itchy, which is surprising because I regularly use concentrated retinol. I

These don't stand out as the best serums I've used, but they're probably the least expensive for average results. Definitely make sure to adjust to the retinol. [5.0/5] just started using it I received this product a while ago and just started to use it a few days ago. It is very nice to apply but not a lot of moisturizing effect. The scent is very subtle. I have used other products like this one with great results for my skin. I have hopes that it will be great [5.0/5] Great set This serum set is a great product and has made my skin smooth, younger looking and overall, much healthier. I will note though, that if you're not used to using retinol, do your research and use it sparingly. It definitely can cause your skin to be dry and flaky at first. I only use it every 3 days or so with this regime and make sure to use moisturizer after I finish applying the serums. I use these serums at night and wash my face gently in the morning and I also use sunscreen if I go out after using retinol as it can cause sunburn. Overall, the benefits of retinol and all of these serums are extremely positive. [5.0/5] Very nice trio for taking care of my skin I'm using the Vitamin C Serum, in the morning, after cleansing my face and before I apply sun screen. In the evening I cleanse my face and then apply Retinol Serum and then the Hyaluronic Acid Serum. I've had no adverse effects. I'm always happy when I can do things to protect and repair my skin because I spend a lot of time outdoors and I'm not young anymore. I do wear hats and sun protection clothing but we have temps over 100 and high, hot winds. Along with making sure to drink fluids, this is another way I can help my skin.

I didn't even notice a scent until I made an effort to do so. It's not bad, it's not good, it's just there and I don't think it is very noticeable at all. [5.0/5] This so far is working. I am 68 and getting compliments for the 1st time. So easy to use. My skin is brighter, clearer and softer.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, most customers are very happy with the Shiny Leaf serum set, which includes Vitamin C, Retinol, and Hyaluronic Acid serums. The most commonly praised features are the pump dispensers instead of droppers, the thicker gel-like consistency that makes application easier, and the affordable price for a three-product set. Many reviewers note that their skin feels smoother, softer, more hydrated, and more youthful after using the products. Several customers with sensitive skin report no irritation, and the set is popular with both beginners and experienced skincare users.

On the downside, a few reviewers mention that the scent is slightly unpleasant, and one customer experienced a serious skin reaction with redness, dryness, and flaking. A couple of reviewers noted that the moisturizing effect felt somewhat limited, and one was uncertain about the concentration of active ingredients. Multiple reviewers recommend using sunscreen when using retinol, starting slowly if you have sensitive skin, and following up with a moisturizer to prevent dryness.

The general consensus is that this set offers great value for the price and delivers solid results, even if it may not be the highest quality option on the market.
